commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a public `openclaw update repair` subcommand and docs guidance that alias hidden update finalization and point plugin-convergence warnings to that recovery path.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +27, Docs +31. Total +75 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature-style CLI recovery-surface PR. Source inspection does show current main only exposes hidden `update finalize` and docs do not list a public `update repair` command.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof for the new public recovery command.

target.title: fix(update): expose plugin convergence repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Add public `openclaw update repair` command that delegates to the existing update finalization path.
- Keep hidden `openclaw update finalize` as an internal alias while documenting repair as the supported operator recovery command.
- Point post-update plugin/convergence guidance at `openclaw update repair` so partial package updates have a direct rerun path for doctor repair, managed npm plugin convergence, install-record metadata, and registry refresh.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm format:fix docs/cli/update.md src/cli/update-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: After a core package update succeeds but post-core plugin convergence or managed npm plugin metadata is stranded, operators now have a documented `openclaw update repair` command instead of discovering the hidden `update finalize` path or rerunning unrelated repair commands.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on macOS using the repo Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The focused update CLI/convergence suite passed with 187 tests, including routing for `update repair`, hidden `update finalize` parity, finalization behavior, and updated post-core guidance.

Observed result after fix: `openclaw update repair` forwards JSON/channel/timeout/yes options to update finalization with restart disabled; post-update plugin warnings now guide operators to `openclaw update repair`; docs describe the recovery command and no-restart behavior.

What was not tested: A live partial package update recovery against real managed plugin npm metadata was not run in this branch.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix(secrets): generate secretref reference docs from the credential matrix
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85969 (replaces the original PR). Adds a shared SecretRef credential-surface Markdown formatter, extends the matrix generator with write/check modes for JSON plus Markdown marker blocks, wires package/preflight scripts, updates generated reference docs, and adds formatter/drift tests.

## Changes from original PR

- **Removed CHANGELOG.md change** — release-owned file
- **Cross-platform scripts** — wrapped `check:secretref-docs` and `gen:secretref-docs` with `node scripts/run-with-env.mjs` instead of POSIX env assignment
- **Rebased onto latest main** — clean cherry-pick

**Behavior or issue addressed**: SecretRef credential matrix and credential-surface docs regenerate cleanly on macOS without POSIX env syntax.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6 (Darwin arm64), Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `8c6e99e82`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Generate JSON matrix + Markdown docs (live macOS run)
pnpm gen:secretref-docs

# Verify docs are in sync (--check mode)
pnpm check:secretref-docs

# Run unit tests for formatter and drift checks
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/secrets/credential-matrix-docs.test.ts
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/secrets/target-registry.docs.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Live macOS generation output:

```
$ pnpm gen:secretref-docs
Wrote docs/reference/secretref-user-supplied-credentials-matrix.json
Updated marker blocks in docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md
```

`check:secretref-docs` confirms no drift:

```
$ pnpm check:secretref-docs
secretref reference docs are in sync.
```

Generated artifacts (live run, actual output):

| File | Lines | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| `secretref-user-supplied-credentials-matrix.json` | 428 | 57 entries |
| `secretref-credential-surface.md` | 126 | 66 config paths documented |

Test suite all pass:

```
 ✓ matrix JSON matches generator output
 ✓ credential-surface marker blocks match generator output
 ✓ matrix and credential-surface marker blocks describe the same registry shape

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

**Observed result after fix**: Both generation and verification work cross-platform on macOS. No POSIX env assignment required. Matrix generation is idempotent (second run shows 'already in sync').

**What was not tested**: Windows and Linux behavior (CI covers these).

@clawsweeper re-review
